articles.title: Very heavy rainfall, dust storm and heatwave warning issued for several states, list released | To read the entire bulletin issued by IMD | Inshorts - Inshorts
articles.full_text:
Very heavy rainfall, dust storm and heatwave warning issued for several states, list released
The IMD has issued rainfall, dust storm and heatwave warning for several states/UTs for Wednesday (June 17). Heavy to very heavy rainfall is very likely over West Bengal, Sikkim, Assam, Meghalaya, Bihar, Kerala, Puducherry and Tamil Nadu. Meanwhile, dust storm is very likely over Rajasthan. Heatwave conditions are very likely over Chhattisgarh, Telangana and Maharashtra.

articles.title: Hottest day of the week brings red flag warning - Oil City News
articles.search_term: ("mercury soars" OR "mercury crosses" OR "temperature soars" OR "record temperature" OR "all-time high" OR "degrees Celsius" OR "maximum temperature" OR "hottest day" OR "temperature crosses 40" OR "temperature crosses 45" OR "temperature likely to rise") Assam
articles.full_text:
CASPER, Wyo. — Hot, dry and windy weather will continue throughout the week, creating higher chances of critical fire danger.
Tuesday will be the hottest day of the week, reaching a high near 89, while strong gusts reaching up to 36 mph will create critical fire weather conditions. A red flag warning is in effect from noon to 9 p.m., the National Weather Service in Riverton states.
Wednesday, a dry cold front will move through overnight, bringing lower temperatures and a high near 82. Strong winds will continue, and fire danger will remain elevated to critical despite the cooler temperatures.
More seasonal temperatures return Thursday before rising back into the upper 80s and low 90s Friday. Dry conditions and low humidity will continue to support elevated fire weather concerns.
The best chance for precipitation arrives Saturday and Sunday. Some storms could produce locally heavy rainfall. Highs will be in the 80s and 90s.
Detailed Forecast
- Today: Mostly cloudy through mid-morning, then gradual clearing, with a high near 89. Breezy, with a north wind at 8–13 mph shifting to the west at 19–24 mph in the morning. Winds could gust as high as 34 mph.
- Tonight: Mostly clear, with a low around 57. Windy, with a west wind at 20–25 mph becoming light. Winds could gust as high as 36 mph.
- Wednesday: Sunny, with a high near 82. Breezy, with a northwest wind at 6–11 mph increasing to 12–17 mph in the afternoon. Winds could gust as high as 28 mph.
- Wednesday Night: Mostly clear, with a low around 46. Breezy, with a north-northeast wind at 16–21 mph calming and shifting to the east-northeast after midnight. Winds could gust as high as 32 mph.
- Thursday: Sunny, with a high near 80. Light and variable wind shifting to the northwest at around 6 mph in the afternoon.
- Thursday Night: Partly cloudy, with a low around 48. East-northeast wind at 6–11 mph becoming light and variable after midnight. Winds could gust as high as 20 mph.
- Juneteenth: Mostly sunny, with a high near 89. Calm wind shifting to the north-northeast at 5–7 mph in the afternoon.
- Friday Night: Partly cloudy, with a low around 55.
- Saturday: A 30% chance of showers and thunderstorms after noon. Mostly sunny, with a high near 89. Breezy.
- Saturday Night: A chance of showers and thunderstorms before midnight, then a slight chance of showers. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 51. Breezy. Chance of precipitation is 30%.
- Sunday: A chance of showers and thunderstorms. Mostly sunny, with a high near 80.
- Sunday Night: A slight chance of showers and thunderstorms. Partly cloudy, with a low around 49.
- Monday: A slight chance of showers and thunderstorms. Mostly sunny, with a high near 81.
